steps.title: Isolate the absolute value
steps.content: Divide both sides of $2|x - 5| = k$ by 2: $$|x - 5| = \frac{k}{2}$$ An absolute value equation $|A| = c$ has: (1) two solutions when $c > 0$; (2) exactly one solution when $c = 0$; (3) no solutions when $c < 0$.

steps.title: Determine k/2 and test all choices
steps.content:
For exactly one solution, we need $\frac{k}{2} = 0$, giving $|x-5| = 0 \implies x = 5$.
  • Choice A — -5 only: $\frac{k}{2} = -5 < 0$ means no solutions. ✗
  • Choice B — -5 or 5: $\frac{k}{2} = -5$ gives no solutions; $\frac{k}{2} = 5 > 0$ gives two solutions. Neither gives exactly one. ✗
  • Choice C — 0 only: $\frac{k}{2} = 0 \implies |x-5| = 0 \implies x = 5$. Exactly one solution. ✓
  • Choice D — 5 only: $\frac{k}{2} = 5 > 0 \implies |x-5| = 5$ gives two solutions $x = 0$ and $x = 10$. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
